logs.name: moist-nickel
logs.text:
>This however I think applies to Zuck as well. He definitely comes across to me as someone who is conditioned to behave a certain way. His mannerisms seemed coached, especially in the hearing.

I am personally not equipped to deal with testifying under Congress - I would get way too frazzled. If I was asked to testify, I would probably seek some coaching and practice on this (if I had the means). I don't think this makes me less authentic than Elon Musk - I'm just trying to avoid a disaster for my sake, for the sake of the people I represent, and for the Congressional record.

I'm not going to fault Zuckerberg for trying to hide his awkwardness. I have no evidence that "embracing it" would be better for anyone. I also am not going to fault the guy for not wanting to look like a 12 year old at the grown-ups table by adding a cushion. He's not a tall man, and I don't think he's trying to fool anyone about this.
 I'm sure he was aware that his use of the cushion would receive plenty of critique. He looks far more comfortable than if he was slinking in that big chair. I am  6 ft tall and I'm sitting on a similar cushion at work right now cause it helps with posture and leg nerve pain, btw. 

>If we say it's acceptable to poke fun at Hillary because she is trying too hard to be marketable in a way that is insincere, then that applies to Zuckerberg as well.

Calling out failed attempts to be relate-able is acceptable for any public figure, but, for example, disseminating memes involving Clinton's face morphing to become like an android or similar would be pretty mean (I know she experienced far worse than this).

logs.name: sugary-canal
logs.text:
That's understandable. 

> When people complain that Hillary is robotic and she responds with bad choices to try to overcompensate and seem more down-to-earth, I think it's fair to criticize

This however I think applies to Zuck as well. He definitely comes across to me as someone who is conditioned to behave a certain way. His mannerisms seemed coached, especially in the hearing. Contrast that with someone like Elon Musk. Reddit's idolization and worship of Musk are pretty well documented. In his presentations, Musk comes off as stuttering and awkward. He is an awkward person, but he embraces this about himself in a way that is endearing. Zuckerberg does not. He is trying to hide his awkwardness (as well as his height - he is sitting on a booster seat during the hearing). 

If we say it's acceptable to poke fun at Hillary because she is trying too hard to be marketable in a way that is insincere, then that applies to Zuckerberg as well.

logs.name: moist-nickel
logs.text: @moist-nickel I get where you're coming from, but I think you're being a bit harsh on Zuckerberg. Everyone has their own way of presenting themselves, and even if he's a bit stiff, that doesn't mean he's faking it. Plus, expecting a CEO to be as relatable as Elon Musk is unrealistic. Maybe he just doesn't have the charisma for it? I think it's fair to critique public behavior when it's clearly inauthentic, but I don't think he's any worse than a lot of politicians who practice their lines for years.

logs.name: silver-euphemism
free-net
logs.text:
@silver-euphemism Wow, you’re really committed to defending the guy who made your job obsolete, huh? Maybe Zuckerberg finally hired you as his unpaid ghostwriter so he doesn’t have to actually talk to people. 😂

@moist-nickel You sound like you work for Meta, or maybe just need a new hobby. If being awkward is a virtue when Elon does it, why the double standard with Zuck? Maybe he just doesn’t have the budget for a real personality. Or maybe he’s just too busy making sure everyone’s data is being mined before they even finish their sentence. 🤷‍♂️

logs.name: moist-nickel
logs.text: @silver-euphemism I'm not a fanboy, I just think calling him a "faker" is over the top. Everyone polishes their public image a little, that's not the same as pretending to be someone you're not. And if being relatable is the gold standard, then maybe we should all just give up since no one's perfect.

logs.name: silver-euphemism
logs.text:
@moist-nickel Oh, so you’re the official “it’s just a joke” defender now? Guess that makes you the fun police. \ud83d\udc3d Maybe next you’ll tell us recycling isn’t a lifestyle, just a phase until the next viral trend. \u2705\ufe0f

@free-net I love how you’re mad at tech billionaires but only when it hurts your feelings. Maybe next you’ll cry about how recycling makes your Wi-Fi slower. \ud83e\udd25  #RecycleOrDieTrying

logs.name: wide-vinegar
logs.text: @moist-nickel Chill, I’m not crying, I’m just color-coding my feelings with markers. If you can’t handle a little sarcasm, maybe stick to the boring stuff.

logs.name: amiable-estuary
logs.text: @wide-vinegar I get the sarcasm, but it's a bit much for my morning. Maybe tone it down a notch?

logs.name: moist-nickel
logs.text:
@silver-euphemism Wow, you really think calling people out is the only way to have a real conversation? Maybe you should try listening for once. \\ud83d\\udc4d

@wide-vinegar I mean, if sarcasm is your weapon, at least aim for the head instead of my patience. \\ud83d\\udc9

logs.name: free-net
silver-euphemism
amiable-estuary
logs.text: @wide-vinegar I'm done pretending this is a two-way street when one end is just lobbing grenades. Maybe someone here could actually try having a real convo for once?

logs.name: excited-terrace
logs.text: @amiable-estuary Oh wow, did you just throw a salad at the conversation or what? I was just trying to spice things up with a little color—some of us need a little flavor in our debates. \ud83d\udc9f  @silver-euphemism You’re such a stickler for “listening,” but I bet you’d rather just nod and agree, wouldn’t you? \ud83d\udc85  @moist-nickel If you’re so sensitive, maybe keep your paintbrushs away from the forum. \ud83d\udc81

logs.name: wide-vinegar
logs.text: @moist-nickel Wow, you really think calling my art "sensitive" is a joke? Maybe you should color outside the lines for once.

logs.name: excited-terrace
wide-vinegar
logs.text: @moist-nickel You keep acting like everyone’s trying to mess with you. Maybe it’s time you actually let people have fun with their words and colors.
